one_time_description: Per capita distribution to members recorded as expense
economic_notes: Nation operates Bison Ranch Project, Golden Lake Cattle, Trappers Program, Broadband Project, Band Store, and has investments in Indigenous Bloom Corp, Megalodon Energy Corp, and Bloom Traditional Tobacco. Five business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method: Woodland Cree Industries Ltd, Woodland Cree Energy Inc, Woodland Cree Exploration Inc, 1024044 Alberta Ltd, and Peace River Logging (50%). Community Development Trust managed by Peace Hills Trust valued at $38.77M.
notes: Audit is qualified on two bases: (1) inability to obtain sufficient audit evidence on carrying value of certain investments including Woodland Cree Industries Ltd, Woodland Cree Energy Inc, and Woodland Cree Exploration Inc whose financial statements were not available at audit date; (2) asset retirement obligations not estimated or recorded per new PS 3280 standard. Five GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. One fully consolidated entity: Woodland Cree First Nation Community Development Trust (~$38.77M). Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($4,048,241) + KTC ($12,199,246) + CMHC ($382,639) + First Nation Development Fund ($1,182,411) + Government of Alberta ($421,957) = $18,254,494. OSR includes interest, investment income, Ottawa trust income, and other revenue. Chief and Council remuneration of $2,510,673 recorded as expense line but individual ranges not disclosed in available text. Per capita distribution of $1,903,500 is a notable one-time item. Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 so fiscal_year = 2023.
